requirements.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list
Notes are displayed with their titles visible
User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes
User can access a search function
Search input field is visible or accessible
User can access an AI chat or assistant feature
Chat interface has an input field for questions
User can see a button or option to create a new note
New note creation is accessible from the main screen
Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy
Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes
Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note
Formatting toolbar or options are available
User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)
App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)
Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile
Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

requirement.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a list of notes categorized in a hierarchical manner, with titles and subtitles to distinguish between main notes and subnotes.
results.notes: Additional context or information about the notes is not visible in this screenshot. It appears to be a basic implementation of a note-taking app that meets the requirement.

requirement.description: Notes are displayed with their titles visible

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there are notes displayed within an application on an iOS device, and their titles are visible. The notes display a title and some additional information such as 'United 12:34 AM' and 'Untitled'. This does not meet the requirement as the note titles should be distinct and easily distinguishable from each other. The titles in the screenshot appear to be duplicated in some cases.
results.notes: Note that the notes section is part of a larger app with other features, such as a 'view notes' button. It might be useful to consider the overall context of the application and user experience when evaluating this requirement.

requirement.description: User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes

results.evidence: In the image, there is a visible sidebar navigation bar on the left-hand side of the app screen that appears to allow users to browse notes. The menu items displayed on the sidebar include 'MCA Classes', 'Entrepreneurship Innovation', 'Venture Capital', and 'Strategy'. There is also an unreadable note or task item at the bottom, which may relate to the requirement. This indicates that the user can navigate through different notes or categories within the app.
results.notes: The sidebar navigation bar seems to be a prominent feature of the app's UI design, making it easy for users to access and browse through their notes.

requirement.description: User can access a search function

results.evidence: In the screenshot, a search function is visible in the top right corner of the app, labeled 'SEARCH'. This meets the requirement of providing access to a search function.
results.notes: The presence of the search function is consistent with standard iOS app design, and its positioning at the top suggests it can be easily accessed by users.

requirement.description: Search input field is visible or accessible

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a search input field labeled 'search', which is visible and accessible.

requirement.description: User can access an AI chat or assistant feature

results.evidence: Screenshot shows an AI chat or assistant feature accessible through a search bar labeled 'AI Assistant' located at the bottom of the app screen, indicating that it is an interactive element which presumably allows users to access AI functionality.
results.notes: The chat icon within the AI Assistant search bar could be more prominently displayed for better usability and user experience.

requirement.description: Chat interface has an input field for questions

results.evidence: In the provided screenshot of an iOS app interface, there is a chat section that includes an input field for users to enter their questions or messages. This input field serves as the place where users can interact with the AI chat feature. Therefore, the requirement 'Chat interface has an input field for questions' is clearly met.
results.notes: The chat interface is designed in such a way that it encourages user interaction and engagement, which is essential for effective communication and support. It is important to consider other aspects of the chat interface such as responsiveness, ease of use, and accessibility in order to ensure a positive user experience.

requirement.description: User can see a button or option to create a new note

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a mobile application interface where a user can see a list of notes. There is a search bar at the top with the placeholder text 'Search'. However, the requirement states that there should be an option to create a new note on the screen. In this image, there does not appear to be any explicit button or option for creating a new note directly from this screen.
results.notes: The navigation menu items suggest that it might be possible to access a 'Create Note' option through one of the menu options. However, the actual functionality and accessibility of this option are not clear from this screenshot alone.

requirement.description: New note creation is accessible from the main screen

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a main screen of an iOS app, and within it, there is a navigation bar at the bottom with different icons, but none of them explicitly correspond to note creation. There is no visible button or action that would allow for the immediate creation of a new note from this main screen.
results.notes: It's unclear if the app supports creating notes directly from this screen without additional navigation or if the note creation functionality may be available elsewhere in the app, which would make it pass as long as there is another way to access it. The presence of a 'Search Notes' field suggests that this feature might be accessed via this search function, but it's not immediately clear from this screenshot.

requirement.description: Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy

results.evidence: The image shows a notes app with nested lists, where one list is indented underneath another, indicating hierarchy and support for nesting.
results.notes: From the screenshot, it's clear that the app allows users to create a nested structure within their notes. This is indicated by the sublist being indented under the main item in the list. The presence of nested lists suggests that the app supports the concept of hierarchy and nesting as per the requirement.

requirement.description: Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a visible hierarchy of notes with indentation to indicate nesting. This clearly meets the requirement for indentation or folder structure being visible for nested notes.
results.notes: The app uses a tree-like layout with indents and icons to differentiate between parent and child notes, making it easy to understand the structure of the notes.

requirement.description: Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note

results.evidence: The image shows a screenshot of an iOS app with a note-taking feature. The editor area is not visible in this view. It seems to be a list view or a category selection screen as indicated by the categories such as 'Venture Capital', 'Strategy', 'Innovation' and others. There is no indication of a text entry field, which would be typical for an editor area.
results.notes: To determine if this requirement is met, I would need to see the app in action, specifically when a user selects a note to edit, to check for the presence of an editor area. The current screenshot does not provide enough information to make that determination.

requirement.description: Formatting toolbar or options are available

results.evidence: In the image provided, it is not possible to determine if a formatting toolbar or options are available in the block editor as there is no visible UI element that indicates a formatting toolbar or options. The screenshot displays an iPhone interface showing a list of options under 'Journals' with different icons and text descriptions such as 'MCA Classes', 'Venture Capital', 'Strategy', 'United', 'Notes', 'Downloads', etc. Each item on the list appears to be a link or a button that presumably leads to another screen for more information or actions. However, none of these options suggest the presence of a formatting toolbar or options for text editing within this particular view.
results.notes: The app could have additional functionality beyond what is visible in this screenshot. For example, if there is an embedded editor in one of the 'Journals' items where users can write and format text, that feature might be accessible through a separate interface not shown in this image. It is important to test the entire user experience to determine if the formatting toolbar or options are available.

requirement.description: User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)

results.evidence: The image shows a block-level content editor on an iOS app. However, only one block is visible in the screenshot: a heading. The paragraphs and list items are not displayed in this view, which does not meet the requirement.
results.notes: Based on what is shown in the screenshot, it appears that the user cannot see multiple blocks at once or edit their content. Further testing would be needed to assess if this is a temporary issue with the interface or an inherent limitation of the feature.

requirement.description: App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)

results.evidence: The image shows a screenshot of an app interface with navigation options at the bottom of the screen labeled 'SEARCH', 'JOBS', and 'PROFILE'. However, the presence of a hamburger menu icon in the top left corner may suggest alternative or additional navigation. It is not clear whether this is part of the standard navigation options or an indication that there is more to explore within the app.
results.notes: To fully assess compliance with the requirement, it would be helpful to see the app in use and understand its full functionality.

requirement.description: Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile

results.evidence: The screenshot shows an iOS app on a mobile device screen. However, the image is taken at a low angle and the touch targets are not clearly visible, making it difficult to accurately assess their sizes in relation to the standard guidelines for appropriate size on mobile devices. To determine if this requirement is met, it would be necessary to have the device in a more upright position with better lighting conditions.
results.notes: To properly evaluate the mobile UI elements in terms of touch targets, an image taken at a higher angle and with better lighting conditions should be used. This will allow for clearer visibility of the app's buttons and other interactive elements to determine if they are appropriately sized.

requirement.description: Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a mobile app screen with content partially cut off and not readable in its current state.
results.notes: There is no clear indication of horizontal scrolling, but the cutoff nature of the image does not allow for a definitive assessment. To properly evaluate this requirement, a fully loaded and uncut-off screenshot would be necessary.
